summary: Provision a new Global Accelerator. Greenfield today (none exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the accelerator shell with its static anycast IPs; listeners and endpoint groups are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new Global Accelerator
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Global Accelerator; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_globalaccelerator_accelerator as a complete specification: name, enabled state and IP address type. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. This provisions the accelerator shell with its static anycast IPs; nothing is routed to until listeners and endpoint groups are added.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Enabled
params.help: Whether the accelerator routes traffic once created.

params.name: ip_address_type
params.label: IP address type
params.help: DUAL_STACK additionally advertises IPv6 anycast addresses.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Accelerator name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this accelerator is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this accelerator.

decisions: Listeners and endpoint groups are engineer follow-ups — this form provisions the accelerator shell with its two static anycast IPs, nothing routed to yet
Flow-log destination (S3 bucket/prefix) and bring-your-own IP address ranges are engineer decisions
